back_translation: And David mustered the people who were with him, and set over them commanders of thousands and commanders of hundreds.

And David sent out the people — a third under the hand of Joab, and a third under the hand of Abishai son of Zeruiah, brother of Joab, and a third under the hand of Ittai the Gittite. And the king said to the people, 'I will surely go out, I also, with you.'

And the people said, 'You shall not go out. For if we surely flee, they will not set their heart on us; and if half of us die, they will not set their heart on us; for now you are as us — ten thousand. And now it is good that you stay to support us from the city.'
And the king said to them, 'Whatever is good in your eyes I will do.' And the king stood beside the gate, and all the people went out by hundreds and by thousands.

And the king commanded Joab and Abishai and Ittai, saying, 'Gently for me with the young man, with Absalom.' And all the people heard when the king commanded all the commanders concerning the matter of Absalom.
And the people went out to the field to meet Israel, and the battle was in the forest of Ephraim.
And the people of Israel were struck down there before the servants of David, and the slaughter was there great on that day — twenty thousand.
And the battle was there scattered over the face of all the land, and the forest devoured more among the people than the sword devoured on that day.

And Absalom was met before the servants of David, and Absalom was riding on the mule, and the mule came under the thick branch of the great oak, and his head caught fast in the oak, and he was given between the heavens and the earth, and the mule which was under him went on.
And one man saw and told Joab, and said, 'Behold, I saw Absalom hanging in the oak.'
And Joab said to the man who was telling him, 'And behold, you saw — and why did you not strike him there to the ground? And it would have been upon me to give to you ten silver and one belt.'

And the man said to Joab, 'And even if I were weighing on my palms a thousand silver, I would not stretch out my hand to the son of the king. For in our ears the king commanded you and Abishai and Ittai, saying, Guard whoever is upon the young man, upon Absalom.
Or if I had acted falsely against my own life — and no matter is hidden from the king — then you yourself would stand aloof.'
And Joab said, 'Not so will I wait before you.' And he took three spears in his hand and thrust them into the chest of Absalom, while he was yet alive in the heart of the oak.
And ten young men, the armor-bearers of Joab, surrounded and struck Absalom and killed him.
And Joab blew the ram's horn, and the people turned back from pursuing after Israel, because Joab restrained the people.
And they took Absalom and threw him in the forest into the great pit, and they raised over him a great heap of stones, very great; and all Israel fled — every man to his tents.

And Absalom had taken and set up for himself, in his lifetime, the pillar that is in the valley of the king, for he said, 'I have no son to cause my name to be remembered.' And he called the pillar after his own name, and it is called 'the hand of Absalom' to this day.
And Ahimaaz son of Zadok said, 'Let me run, please, and bring good news to the king — that the LORD has vindicated him from the hand of his enemies.'
And Joab said to him, 'You are not a man of good news today. But you shall bring news on another day. And today you shall not bring news, because the son of the king is dead.'
And Joab said to the Cushite, 'Go, tell the king what you have seen.' And the Cushite bowed down to Joab and ran.

And Ahimaaz son of Zadok added again and said to Joab, 'And let it be whatever — please let me also run, even I, behind the Cushite.' And Joab said, 'Why is this — you running, my son? — and for you there is no good news finding.'
'And let it be whatever, I will run.' And he said to him, 'Run.' And Ahimaaz ran by the way of the plain and outran the Cushite.
And David was sitting between the two gates, and the watchman went up to the roof of the gate, to the wall, and lifted up his eyes and saw, and behold — a man running alone.
And the watchman called and told the king. And the king said, 'If alone, good news is in his mouth.' And he went on going and drawing near.
And the watchman saw another man running, and the watchman called to the gatekeeper and said, 'Behold — a man running alone.' And the king said, 'Also this one is a bringer of good news.'
And the watchman said, 'I see the running of the first one like the running of Ahimaaz son of Zadok.' And the king said, 'A good man is this, and with good news he comes.'

And Ahimaaz called and said to the king, 'Peace.' And he bowed down to the king with his face to the ground; and he said, 'Blessed be the LORD your God, who has shut up the men who lifted their hand against my lord the king.'
And the king said, 'Peace to the young man, to Absalom?' And Ahimaaz said, 'I saw the great tumult when Joab sent the king's servant and your servant, but I do not know what.'
And the king said, 'Turn aside, stand here.' And he turned aside and stood.
And behold, the Cushite came, and the Cushite said, 'Let my lord the king receive good news, for the LORD has vindicated you today from the hand of all those who rose against you.'
And the king said to the Cushite, 'Peace to the young man, to Absalom?' And the Cushite said, 'May the enemies of my lord the king and all who rose against you for evil be like the young man.'
